findings.quote: Because municipal glass can never be cleaned well enough to melt without flaws under any circumstances whatever.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Option C is an extreme absolute eliminable by form, and the stem's clean-vs-municipal framing entails that the contamination rides in with the dirty glass (option B), giving a passage-blind reader with no domain knowledge a material edge. A and D remain substantive mechanisms, so not decided blind. blind_pick B.

findings.quote: Convinced: she shares Feld's certainty that there is no such thing as feeding a furnace too much recycled glass.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Options A and C both express agreement with Feld (redundant same-direction pair) and D is an extreme dismissal; the qualified option B survives by form as the lone nuanced middle. A vs B still needs the passage. blind_pick B.

findings.quote: Pooling the logs made each individual voyage less accurate than a single careful reckoning would have been on its own.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Option D is logically self-undermining (averaging cannot retroactively make one past voyage less accurate) and option A is an extreme ('learn nothing useful'); both fall by form, leaving B/C. Materially better than chance; passage still needed to choose C. blind_pick C.

findings.quote: What does Brandt's distinction between a 'more accurate' voyage and a 'less wrong' collective estimate imply?
findings.note: PARTIALLY. The stem restates the very distinction the key encodes; options B and C are extremes eliminable by form, and D captures both halves of the stem's contrast better than the partial A. Near stem-leak, but A stays stem-consistent so not cleanly decided to one. blind_pick D.

findings.quote: Convinced: the writer fully endorses the standard story that better instruments, above all else, were what made the coast safe.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Options A ('fully endorses') and C ('a myth with no evidence') are opposite extremes eliminable by form; B is the qualified survivor. Test-wise edge, but B vs D (detached) needs the passage. blind_pick B.

findings.quote: Warm loading docks were shown to ruin the great majority of every operator's deliveries throughout the region across the year.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Option D is an extreme over-generalisation ('every operator... throughout the region') and B is operationally odd (reefer doors open while driving on a motorway); both fall by form, leaving A vs C as direct opposites the passage must decide. blind_pick C.

findings.quote: Man bör varken förhärliga badhusen som en förlorad gemenskap eller avfärda dem som enbart primitiva reliker.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Classic balanced-attitude key: A ('borde återuppföras') and C ('nostalgin saknar all grund') are extremes and D carries an odd editorial tail; B survives by form as the neither/nor middle. Edge without domain knowledge, but passage confirms. blind_pick B.

findings.quote: Var snön samlas påverkar smältvattnets kvalitet, fastän nyttan är begränsad och delvis omtvistad.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. A is the only hedged option among three overstatements — C ('största... alla sjöar'), D ('ofarligt'), and B a motive claim — so the qualified survivor is pickable by form. Distractors substantive enough that the passage still grounds it. blind_pick A.

findings.quote: Att ojämna amatöranteckningar ändå är en vetenskaplig tillgång som borde bevaras innan de går förlorade.
findings.note: PARTIALLY. Options A/B/C all diminish or redirect the amateur record's scientific value while D alone affirms it — a 3-1 semantic cluster visible from form. A blind reader leans D; the three remain substantive stances a passage grounds, so flag not kill. blind_pick D.
